title: Modular Office Narrow Drawer Base Unit
summary: A narrow two-drawer plywood pedestal designed as one interchangeable module in a modular office system — genuinely small enough to slot under a desktop alongside other matching base units.
description:
This is a single module meant to sit alongside other matching bases under a shared desktop, rather than a standalone desk — a narrow plywood pedestal holding two drawers, sized to be genuinely interchangeable with other units in the same modular system. **If you want to finish the front edges of the plywood with edge banding, do that before assembly, not after** — the plan specifically calls out doing this early since only the side front edges need it, and it's far easier to iron on banding to a flat panel than to a partially assembled cabinet.

Attach the 1x boards to the plywood sides with pocket holes if you have a jig, or self-tapping screws and glue if you don't — clamping the boards down while attaching helps if you're working solo without an extra set of hands. The top attaches with brad nails and glue, all outside edges kept flush.

**Drawer depth is a genuine build-time choice**: use 1x10s instead of the plan's 1x8s for taller drawer sides, or 1x6s to save a little money at the cost of shorter sides — pick your depth before cutting rather than mixing sizes within one drawer. Install the drawer members on the slides with the drawer box inset 3/4" from the cabinet's front edge, test the fit before final attachment, and adjust now if anything binds — this is much easier to fix before the drawer face goes on than after.

tools.note: Holding boards in place while attaching solo.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, half sheet or 8 ft strip
Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping wood screws, 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, half sheet or 8 ft strip
3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, half sheet or 8 ft strip

cutList.note: Cut to fit; positioned in a later step.

steps.title: Edge band and assemble the sides
steps.body: If finishing plywood edges with edge banding, apply it now to the side front edges. Attach the 1x boards to the plywood sides with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, or 2" self-tapping screws and glue.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, half sheet or 8 ft strip
Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping wood screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, keeping all outside edges flush.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, half sheet or 8 ft strip

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Attach the bottoms with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Choose 1x8, 1x10, or 1x6 stock for drawer sides depending on desired depth.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping wood screws, 2"

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Install the drawer members of the slides on the bottom of the drawers, inset 3/4" from the cabinet's front edge. Install the cabinet members inside the cabinet.

steps.title: Test fit and adjust
steps.body: Test the drawer slide fit. Adjust now if anything binds, making sure everything slides smoothly.

steps.title: Attach the drawer face
steps.body: With the cabinet upright, position the drawer face on the front and attach with a few 1-1/4" brad nails, avoiding fastener locations that would interfere with slides.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Narrow two-drawer modular office base unit
